judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent grounding and correctness; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: Batch B — registry (F-02, F-05, F-09). Owns `pipeline/registry.py`. All three findings edit this one 11-line file, so they must belong to one engineer

report_2.evidence: Engineer C — `pipeline/registry.py` (F-02, F-05, F-09). Three one-line changes to one 12-line file... Splitting these across people would be three PRs conflicting on adjacent lines for trivial gain.

report_1.evidence: Ordering: all four batches run **in a single parallel wave**, because after the by-file grouping they are fully file-disjoint... The fix and its edge-case test... go to the same engineer, test-first... with the F-12 boundary test written first

report_2.evidence: all four streams start in parallel on day one, branching from `f3cac92` on `main`, because the file sets are disjoint... Engineer B — `pipeline/rubric.py` (F-01, F-11)... Engineer C — `pipeline/registry.py` (F-02, F-05, F-09)

report_1.evidence: F-06 — escalate, don't assign. "Tighten `PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS` so borderline answers score lower" is a deliberate change to scoring output — user-facing behavior... Whether and how much to tighten is a product/eval-owner decision, not something an engineer should guess. Flag it as a decision request

report_2.evidence: F-06 — a policy decision disguised as a bug... Changing it silently changes every score the pipeline produces. This needs the reviewer or a product owner to specify the new values... before anyone codes it

report_1.evidence: the default in `tests/conftest.py:4` is the shared `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db` and parallel suite runs against it would collide... each needs an isolated override

report_2.evidence: A per-engineer `RESULTS_DB` environment variable pointing at a private path, since `tests/conftest.py` defaults to the machine-global `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`... Engineers running the suite concurrently on the same host would share (and could clobber) one results database unless each overrides `RESULTS_DB`
